claims.statement: Query-adaptive arbitration improves headline retrieval top-1 accuracy on the bounded widened teacher-anchor surface.
claims.scope: One fixed widened teacher-anchor retrieval surface with the upstream packet bundle, evaluation harness, and consumer setup held fixed.

evidence.support: Top-1 accuracy rises to 0.15625 from 0.125 for raw-global and 0.09375 for temporal-context on the same bounded surface.
On the repaired broader three-bundle surface, query-adaptive still keeps the best non-oracle top-1 at 0.125 versus 0.10416666666666667 for both static parent routes.

limitations: Temporal-context remains stronger on chunk-level top-1 accuracy (0.375 vs 0.25) on the main bounded surface.

On the repaired broader three-bundle surface, temporal-context still has better mean_match_rank (7.8125 vs 8.479166666666666) and chunk_top1_accuracy (0.3333333333333333 vs 0.25), so the broader claim remains intentionally partial.
A first low-capacity downstream-consumer bridge smoke on the recovered chunked teacher-packet bundle was negative, so the manuscript does not claim broader consumer transfer.
No larger downstream consumer is reported yet.

claims.statement: The frozen teacher-anchor packet surface contains downstream-recoverable structure that static routing underuses on the same bounded surface.
claims.scope: Interpretation is limited to the same frozen packet bundle and the same bounded retrieval surface.

evidence.support: The adaptive rule improves top-1 accuracy while reducing hub-cluster share from 0.59375 to 0.28125 without changing the upstream packet representation.

The repaired broader follow-up keeps the headline top-1 edge for query-adaptive but shows that temporal-context remains the stronger ranking-side control, which sharpens the interpretation boundary instead of erasing the downstream-readable signal.

limitations: This is evidence about downstream readout over a fixed packet surface, not a new packet schema or coding method.
The broader repaired three-bundle validation still does not justify a claim of uniform route superiority beyond the bounded paper surface.
A first low-capacity downstream-consumer bridge smoke was negative and is treated only as a future-work boundary note.
An appendix-level wider-surface QB-Norm revalidation improved geometry without improving top-1 retrieval, so broader route-stability claims remain unsupported.
